---
title: "Marcelo Ortiz M."
image: profile.png
toc: false
from: markdown+emoji
about: 
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  image-width: 30em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/marcelo-ortiz-m-02527328/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/mortizm1988
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://x.com/MarceloOrtizM1
    - icon: google
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ABf2QTQAAAAJ&hl=en&authuser=1
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:marcelo.ortizm@upf.edu
---

I'm an Assistant Professor at [Universitat Pompeu Fabra](https://www.upf.edu/en/web/universitat/la-upf-en-els-ranquings), and affiliated with the Barcelona School of Economics and the Barcelona School of Management.

My research in **corporate governance** examines how regulatory interventions, such as reporting requirements and corporate taxation, affect corporate strategy and market outcomes, with implications for **accounting, finance, and management**. My work has been published in leading journals, including three publications in FT50 journals.

**Research areas**: Corporate Governance, Financial Reporting, Corporate Taxation, Private Firms.

I also hold a **Ramon y Cajal Fellowship** from the Spanish Ministry for Science and Innovation, and co-organize the [Barcelona Accounting Summer Workshops](https://sites.google.com/view/bas-2021/inicio).

[CV link](cv_MOrtiz.pdf).

**Spotlight:**

-   I will be serving as an **Academic Visitor** at the Saïd Business School, **University of Oxford**, this spring.
-   **New Working Paper**: [Board Gender Quotas and Female CEOs in Private Firms](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5747103).
-   **Updated Working Paper**: [The Effect of Mandatory Disclosure on the CEO Labor Market](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5052279).
-   **Policy Impact**: Our findings on the role of [transparency thresholds for M&As](https://academic.oup.com/rfs/article-abstract/36/12/4788/7202058?redirectedFrom=fulltext&login=false) (*Review Financial Studies*, 2023) were incorporated into the discussion of **IFRS 3 Business Combinations** by the [UK Endorsement Board](https://ifrs-springapps-comment-letter-api-1.azuremicroservices.io/v2/download-file?path=638_68180_UK-Endorsement-Board_0_UKEB-Final-Comment-Letter-ED-BCDGI-UNSIGNED.pdf) and analyzed in an [IASB Meeting](https://www.ifrs.org/content/dam/ifrs/meetings/2025/april/iasb/ap18c-pis-evidence-based-feedback.pdf).
-   **Upcoming presentations**: Warwick Business School, Lancaster University, EAA Annual Congress 2026, SIOE 2026, FMARC 2026.
-   **Recent presentations**: SKEMA Business School, CUNEF University, St. Gallen Financial Economics Workshop, PUC-Chile, Paris-Dauphine Family Firm Seminars.
